Let's review the evidence again focusing on why induction agents stand out as primary causes during this critical phase. Induction agents such as propofol and suxamethonium can trigger significant immune responses because they often require rapid administration which may lead to faster onset of symptoms if there’s any underlying allergy. Given their frequent use at the beginning of surgery, it makes sense that these would be associated with many reported cases of acute allergic reactions immediately post-administration.
